summary: Planning phase (Stages 1 - 4.5) complete for epistemic-session-race blueprint. Spec at rev2 with 15 WUs and ~50 findings absorbed across critique pipeline. Ready to resume at Stage 6 (Test → /spec-to-tests) then Stage 7 (Execute) in a fresh session for clean context window.
decisions: Marker scheme (rev2): per-claude-PID file, claude PID resolved via process-tree traversal in shared scripts/epistemic-marker.sh helper. Decided against session_id-keyed marker + by-PID index — more moving parts for no correctness gain. Probe v2 archived as evidence.
Process-tree traversal MUST parse /proc/$PID/status PPid line, NOT /proc/$PID/stat field 4 (E1 patch — comm names with spaces/parens corrupt field-position parsing).
Atomic write tmpfile MUST land on same filesystem as marker (E2 patch — mktemp -p $(dirname "$marker_path")).
uuidgen fallback REMOVED entirely (CF-7). If stdin parse fails, hook continues fail-open with stderr warning, no marker written, no fallback.

Source-branching in WU2: startup → write fresh marker; resume → unconditionally write new marker (NOT 'reuse if matches' — prior marker already deleted by SessionEnd); clear/unrecognized → treat as startup with stderr warning. CRITICAL: do NOT auto-resubmit preflight on resume/clear (NEW-2 constraint).

WU5 split into 5a/b/c/d for per-file consumer fixes (rev1 underspecified). Each consumer site gets explicit fix type: rewrite create-fallback (5a), rewrite Step 6 cleanup (5b), rewrite line-284 grep that doesn't match UUIDs (5c), replace -f directory checks with epistemic_session_active (5d).

Stage 3 critique used Standard tier (Orient + 3 Diverge + Interaction Scan + 3 Clash + Converge). REWORK on rev1 (4 critical findings) → spec rewritten → READY on rev2 (19/20 fixed, 4 minor new patches absorbed inline).
Stage 4 (Edge Cases) applied 6 inline patches (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6, E8) — no architectural rework needed.

Stage 4.5 (Pre-Mortem) absorbed PM-Fix-1 (post-install verification) and PM-Fix-3 (marker visibility in /start) into existing WUs. PM-Fix-2 (epistemic.json health probe) deferred to follow-up blueprint. PM-2 (tmpfs reboot loss) accepted as known limitation.

Stage 5 (Review) skipped with reason: ~50 findings already absorbed across earlier critique stages; reviewer plugin profiles don't match shell-hook refactor work shape; diminishing returns. /gpt-review remains available if desired before Stage 7.
next_action: On resume in fresh session: run `/blueprint epistemic-session-race` to load manifest. Workflow will surface checkpoint and resume at Stage 6 (Test). Run `/spec-to-tests` to generate spec-blind tests, THEN advance to Stage 7 (Execute). Critical path: WU1 (helper) → WU2 (preflight refactor) → WU3 (postflight) in batch 1 → WU4/WU5a-d in parallel (or sequential per simplicity preference) → WU8/WU9/WU10/WU11 evals (sequential due to evals.json file conflict) → WU7 smoke test → WU12 test.sh extensions.

epistemic.session_id_marker_status: CLOBBERED — do NOT read ~/.claude/.current-session for session_id at /end of THIS session. Marker was overwritten by parallel claude session at 17:08:43Z on 2026-05-01.

operational_notes_for_next_session: Loading via /blueprint epistemic-session-race will surface this checkpoint via session-sail hook.
On resume, READ manifest.json (token-dense recovery format) — do NOT re-read full markdown artifacts unless current stage's work requires them.
Stage 6 next: /spec-to-tests is meant to run BEFORE seeing implementation (spec-blind). Resist the urge to peek at hooks/epistemic-preflight.sh before generating test cases.
Stage 7 execution: WU1 is highest-leverage (helper file is the foundation). Critical path WU1 → WU2 → WU8.
Eval simulation (WU8) carries known risk: subshell-with-PPID-propagation is the planned approach but worked example wasn't drafted in spec. Allow extra time.

Three confirmed parallel-session incidents on 2026-05-01 — new sessions you start while testing this blueprint WILL collide markers until WU1+WU2 are deployed. Use snapshot pattern documented in RECOVERY-INSTRUCTIONS.md if you /end any session before this fix is in place.
